assumptions: Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Checks are limited to reset-safe, protocol-safe, or output-visible behavior.
No fixed latency or exact cycle count is assumed.
dout updates combinationally with any change on addr
Output is deterministic for a given addr value
cases.goal: Confirm that any address yields a deterministic dout value as defined by the ROM contents
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
No timing assumptions beyond combinational propagation are made
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Only deterministic, contract-supported stimulus is used; otherwise the case is downgraded.
Drive addr with arbitrary values and monitor dout for deterministic response
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Observe dout in the same delta cycle after addr changes, reflecting combinational logic

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
Address 0x00 returns the ROM entry at index 0
Address 0xFF returns the ROM entry at index 255
cases.goal: Exercise min and max address values to ensure correct ROM indexing
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Ensures that the address decoder correctly handles the full 8‑bit range without overflow
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['addr']
Apply addr=0x00 and addr=0xFF and verify corresponding dout values
timing_assumptions: Use reset-safe or protocol-safe observation windows only.
Observe dout immediately after each boundary addr stimulus, respecting combinational behavior

expected_properties: dout reflects the ROM content at the supplied addr without spurious changes
Repeated reads of the same address produce identical dout values
cases.goal: Validate combinational read consistency for arbitrary address sequences
notes: Leverages the combinational nature of the ROM; no clock or reset needed
Observations are made in the same simulation delta cycle after addr stimulus
preconditions: addr input is driven with a defined 8‑bit value
stimulus_intent: Apply a sequence of address values and verify that dout matches the expected ROM contents
timing_assumptions: Assume zero‑delay combinational propagation; dout can be sampled immediately after addr changes

expected_properties: Address 0x00 yields the ROM entry at index 0
Address 0xFF yields the ROM entry at index 255
cases.goal: Exercise the extreme address values of the ROM address space
notes: Confirms that the address decoder correctly handles the full 8‑bit range
preconditions: addr input can be driven with 0x00 and 0xFF
stimulus_intent: Drive addr with its minimum and maximum possible values and monitor dout
timing_assumptions: Observe dout immediately after each addr stimulus, respecting combinational behavior

unresolved_items: Timing model is unresolved; generated cases avoid fixed-latency checks.
Contract strength is limited; generated cases favor safe observation over precise timing assumptions.
Negative or illegal-input behavior is not planned because the contract does not provide reliable constraints.
